We must acknowledge that the decision by the SACP to contest elections separately threatens the unity and cohesion of the Alliance.
 https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

#ANCNGC https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

To foster a culture of ethics and integrity, the #ANCNEC strengthened the terms of reference of the Integrity Commission, and introduced a compulsory course on ethics and integrity in the political education curriculum.

#ANCNGC 
https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

In fulfilment of the #ANC55 mandate, the NEC implemented clear accountability mechanisms to tie leadership to expectations, measurable performance and consistent reporting.
 https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

We require a renewal of values and ethics. We need to deal decisively with corruption and unacceptable conduct. And we must forge a cadreship imbued with a militant, principled and fighting spirit.
 
#ANCNGC https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

Renewal requires that we reaffirm the organisational culture and traditions of the ANC as a movement rooted among the people, representing their interests, concerns and aspirations.
#ANCNGC https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

The theme of our NGC asks delegates to interrogate how the ANC must be united, strengthened and renewed to respond to the challenges and opportunities of our time.

#ANCNGC 
https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

During this NGC, delegates are called upon to reflect on 30 years of democracy, assess the balance of forces and the current conjuncture, characterise the nature of the crises we face and the implications for the NDR, and determine what should be done.
 https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

The fulfilment of the aspirations set out in the #FreedomCharter remains the central objective of the National Democratic Revolution.
 https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

This year marks the 70th anniversary of the #FreedomCharter, which declared that our country would not grow and our people would not prosper until all of us live in a united, non-racial, non-sexist, democratic and prosperous South Africa.

https://t.co/JQqUg7QVRF

Dear Fellow South African,
 
As South Africa and the world observes 16 Days of Activism against Gender-Based Violence from 25 November to 10 December, it is a shame that our country has the dubious distinction of having one of the world’s highest levels of violence against women https://t.co/WHs2OR0Wsr

My thoughts are with Shadley Lombard and siblings Chevan and Yana as they bid farewell to their beloved mother, trade unionist and struggle activist Colleen Lombard.

Colleen Lombard has left us at the age of 75, following a long illness, during which she lost her husband,
Presidential Climate Commission Launch of 5th Year Legacy Report https://t.co/WRzWDaLUWl
Youth Employment Service (YES) 200k Jobs Celebration  https://t.co/Vz6cAUdlBn

GBV will fall when men rise. Not in violence, but in respect, courage and accountability.

 https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

It does not help the cause against gender-based violence that we are only intervening once perpetrators have already entered the criminal justice system; prevention efforts must begin in the family, and in communities.

https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

The toxic masculinity that is driving the abuse of women and children is a direct by-product of the violent society in which we live.

https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

The abusers of women and children live among us. They are fathers, our sons, our grandfathers, uncles, cousins and friends.

https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

There are the older men and men in positions of authority throwing their money around in nightclubs and functions to practically buy young girls that they derogatorily refer to as ‘istocko.’ 

https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

Abuse doesn’t only refer to bruises. 
There are men preying on girls young enough to be their daughters, buying groceries for the childrens’ families to buy their silence.
 https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

Relationships have become a battleground. According to a survey on GBV published last year, a third of South African women have experienced physical violence in their lifetime, and most of the cases involve intimate partners. https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

Many men in this country are suffering from depression from being unemployed, from isolation and lonelinessbeing separated from their families, and struggling with alcoholism and drug dependency.
https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

The presence of a father in the home is important to shaping the attitudes and behaviour of boys, and that so many young boys in South Africa lack father figures is heartbreaking. https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

The vast majority of children in South Africa don’t live with their biological fathers. This is a terrible tragedy.
 https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

This Men’s Indaba is a clear statement that we are no longer outsourcing the fight against GBV to women who are the victims. 

https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

Let us not think that our silence does not mean our complicity. Let us not assume that by looking away as another man abuses a woman we are not guilty too – because this crime continues to fester in a culture of silence.

https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

I am not here to criticise all men. 

We are here because we see the men of our country as being part of the solution.

https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

Gender-based violence is the actions of men, and about the terrible crimes they commit. In the name of culture and tradition. In the name of proving their masculinity. In the name of all forms of justifications.

As I have said many times in the past, gender-based violence is not a problem of women. 

 https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

Every day across this country, the safety and dignity of women and children  is being undermined by the actions of violent men.

https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

You are here because you recognise that violence against women and children is a pandemic, and a crisis that will destroy our nation if we do not stop it.

 https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

To see you all gathered here; men from all walks of life, young and not so young, fills me with pride.

https://t.co/Ud9W8fy0Dh

 https://t.co/HRacDDLPoI

https://t.co/HRacDDLPoI

This is blatant misinformation about

It is regrettable that the United States of America, which is a founding member of the G20 and which takes over the Presidency of the G20 from South Africa in 2026, chose not to participate in the G20 Leaders’ Summit and the final meetings leading up to it.

All G20 members attended all the G20 related meetings throughout the year except for one member country.

 https://t.co/HRacDDLPoI
